one_line_takeaway: Watermarks baked into open-weight LLMs survive quantization and pruning but are reliably erased by model merging and especially by a few hundred steps of finetuning, so the paper formalizes 'durability' as the missing requirement and shows no existing open-source-model watermark meets it.
key_finding: No current OSM watermark is durable across all common modifications. Quantization (even 4-bit) and pruning up to rho=0.5 are largely survived by all schemes (TPR stays high, e.g. KGW-D 0.98-1.00). Durability collapses under SLERP merging (at t=0.5 KGW-D falls to 0.50, KTH-D 0.15, GAUSSMARK 0.68; by t=0.9 all near-zero) and, most damningly, under full finetuning on broad-domain OPENWEBTEXT: after only 500 steps KGW-D TPR drops to 0.35 and to 0.22 after 2500 steps, with every scheme becoming useless. Weight-editing watermarks (UNREMOVABLE, GAUSSMARK) are more merge-durable than distillation-based ones; UNREMOVABLE resists LoRA finetuning better because it hides in a last-layer bias LoRA does not touch. Task-specific OPENMATHINSTRUCT finetuning is survived better (especially LoRA), but strength then degrades specifically on the finetuned domain. The GPT-2 study shows durability scales with the amount of watermarked training exposure, and pretraining-from-scratch distillation is 'task-aware' (up to ~20% higher TPR retention against task-specific finetuning) but still insufficient against prolonged broad-domain finetuning.
core_contribution: The paper provides the first systematic study of watermarking OPEN-SOURCE LLMs (OSM), where users have white-box access and can bypass any generation-time (decoding-side) watermark. Its own contributions are: (1) re-interpreting the four established generation-time watermark requirements (quality, robustness, undetectability, security) for the OSM setting and arguing security/spoofing is NOT a fundamental OSM concern; (2) introducing a NEW requirement, DURABILITY, i.e. resistance of a weight-embedded watermark to common non-adversarial model modifications (quantization, pruning, SLERP merging, finetuning), together with a concrete, reproducible evaluation procedure; (3) surveying and systematically evaluating all current OSM watermarks under this procedure and proposing one new variant (KGW-D+CTV, based on contrastive task vectors), concluding NO existing method is truly durable; and (4) a GPT-2 proof-of-concept showing that scaling watermark-distillation training data improves durability and that distillation-during-pretraining is 'task-aware'. Note: the taxonomy of prior OSM watermarks (distillation-based KGW-D/KTH-D from Gu et al. 2024, RL-based from Xu et al. 2024, weight-editing UNREMOVABLE from Christ et al. 2024a and GAUSSMARK from Block et al. 2025) is CITED prior work that they evaluate, not their own scheme; their only novel construction is KGW-D+CTV and the pretraining/long-distillation GPT-2 experiments.
threat_model: Setting: a model provider wants to embed a traceable watermark directly into open-weight LLM parameters so that all outputs remain attributable. Because open-source users control the full decoding process, generation-time watermarks are trivially disabled, so the watermark must live in the weights (gw: theta,key -> theta). The primary 'threat' to durability is NOT a dedicated remover but the ecosystem of common model modifications that third-party users routinely apply and redistribute (Hugging Face hosts ~200k models, 200M+ downloads): quantization, pruning, model merging, and finetuning. These are mostly non-adversarial but a motivated party can also use them (or merging/finetuning specifically) to strip the watermark. The paper explicitly argues that spoofing/stealing (a core generation-time 'security' requirement) is NOT a fundamental issue for OSM since only a single fixed model is released and jailbreaking already lets users produce harmful watermarked text; undetectability is noted as an open concern (distillation-based OSM watermarks inherit detectability of the underlying generation-time watermark).
limitations: The proof-of-concept durability improvement is only demonstrated at GPT-2 scale, so generalization to large capable models is unclear and the authors flag scaling as expensive. Even ~9B-token distillation does not make the watermark survive prolonged broad-domain finetuning, so no practically-durable method is delivered. The RL-based watermark (Xu et al. 2024) and distilled AAR were omitted because the authors could not train them to sufficient quality, leaving those methods unevaluated. Only LLAMA2-7B and one architecture family are used for the main evaluation; only SLERP merging and unstructured pruning are covered (structured pruning excluded because it needs recovery finetuning). The 5% FPR operating point is acknowledged as high for real deployment. Undetectability of the schemes is not guaranteed.

datasets_used: C4 RealNewsLike split (prompts: first 50 tokens of each entry; generation of completions)
OPENWEBTEXT (broad-domain finetuning modification and distillation data)
quantitative_results: Unaltered TPR@5%FPR (LLAMA2-7B): KGW-D 0.99, KTH-D 0.81, UNREMOVABLE 0.98, GAUSSMARK 0.99, KGW-D+CTV 0.99
4-bit NF4 quantization TPR: KGW-D 0.98, KTH-D 0.77, UNREMOVABLE 0.99, GAUSSMARK 0.97 (all schemes remain durable to quantization)
Pruning SPARSEGPT rho=0.5 TPR: KGW-D 1.00, KTH-D 0.89, GAUSSMARK 0.94 (durable up to 0.5 sparsity)
SLERP merge t=0.5 TPR: KGW-D 0.50, KTH-D 0.15, UNREMOVABLE 0.93, GAUSSMARK 0.68, KGW-D+CTV 0.59
SLERP merge t=0.9 TPR: KGW-D 0.04, KTH-D 0.09, UNREMOVABLE 0.35, GAUSSMARK 0.10 (near collapse)
Full finetuning on OPENWEBTEXT: KGW-D TPR 0.35 at 500 steps, 0.22 at 2500 steps; no scheme durable (KTH-D 0.09/0.10, GAUSSMARK 0.48/0.29)
LoRA finetuning on OPENWEBTEXT: UNREMOVABLE retains TPR 0.94 at 500 steps but drops to 0.62 at 2500 steps
LoRA finetuning on OPENMATHINSTRUCT (task-specific) is best survived: KGW-D 0.99 at both 500 and 2500 steps
GPT-2 proof-of-concept, finetune 2500 steps on OPENMATHINSTRUCT TPR@5: KGW-D(PRETRAINED) 0.96, KGW-D(LONG) 0.91, KGW-D(STANDARD) 0.66
GPT-2 proof-of-concept, finetune 2500 steps on OPENWEBTEXT TPR@5: KGW-D(PRETRAINED) 0.79, KGW-D(LONG) 0.84, KGW-D(STANDARD) 0.73
Pretraining-from-scratch distillation shows up to ~20% higher TPR retention than LONG on task-specific finetuning, at most ~10% lower on broad-domain
Evaluation protocol: 100 completions of length 200 (1000 for GPT-2 study); PPL scored by LLAMA3-8B
baselines_compared: KGW-D (distillation of KGW watermark, Gu et al. 2024) - prior work
KTH-D (distillation of KTH watermark, Gu et al. 2024) - prior work
UNREMOVABLE (last-layer Gaussian bias, Christ et al. 2024a) - prior work
GAUSSMARK (Gaussian noise on a weight subset, Block et al. 2025) - prior work
KGW-D+CTV (contrastive-task-vector variant) - the paper's own new variant
RL-based watermark (Xu et al. 2024) - surveyed but omitted from evaluation due to insufficient text quality
